Rule.name: WorkItem Forward Rule

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The WorkItem being opened or forwarded.

Argument.name: owner
Argument.Description: The Identity that currently owns the work item.

Argument.name: newOwner
Argument.Description: An Identity that should own the work item. Alternatively, this can be a String that is the name or id of the Identity.

Signature:
SailPoint rule ka method definition hota hai. Isme bataya jata hai:- Rule ko kya input milega Rule kya return karega Aapke rule ki signature:
Haan. context SailPoint automatically provide karta hai jab rule execute hota hai. Aapko manually create nahi karna padta. Ye line:-
batati hai ki execution time par SailPoint ek SailPointContext object pass karega. Isi tarah:
ye bhi SailPoint runtime se automatically milte hain. Isliye rule ke andar directly use kar sakte ho: *///////// //////////* ():-Ek input parameter define ho raha hai --name="item" Is parameter ka naam item hai. --“SailPoint rule ko item naam ka ek object dega.”
Ye item kya hota hai? → Current WorkItem Example:- Access approval Password reset approval Certification decision Manual provisioning task Agar kisi user ke paas approval pending hai, wahi object item hota hai.
Haan, basically input variable define karta hai. Example:-
matlab:- ek variable ban raha hai variable ka naam = item value SailPoint dega
()Manual Provisioning Task kya hota hai? Jab SailPoint automatically account/access provide nahi kar pata, tab manual task create hota hai. Example:- User ko SAP access dena hai Lekin SAP connector configured nahi hai To SailPoint kisi admin ke paas manual work bhejta hai
Jaise:- “Please create SAP account manually for Ravi” Ye jo pending task hai wahi Manual Provisioning Task hai. Ye ek WorkItem hota hai.
()owner kya hota hai? owner = current WorkItem ka owner Yani jis user/workgroup ke paas abhi task assigned hai. Example:- Approval task currently Ravi ke paas hai To:- owner = Ravi Identity object.
()
iska matlab:- Rule ek value return karega aur us return value ka naam newOwner hai.
Simple language:- SailPoint rule se poochta hai:- "WorkItem ab kiske paas jaana chahiye?"
Rule answer deta hai:- return newOwner;
()Aapke code me:- Identity newOwner = workGroupIdentity; matlab:- newOwner = Admin Fir last me:- return newOwner; matlab:- "SailPoint, is WorkItem ko Admin ko de do" *////////////
